history.text: Archaeological finds at Semermiut, near Ilulissat, show that the area was inhabited by Inuit peoples from prehistoric times, with evidence of Saqqaq settlement around 1500 BC and Dorset occupation from about 300 AD.

history.label: 18th century
history.text: The Zion Church (Zions Kirke) was built in the late 18th century; it was the largest man-made structure in Greenland at the time.
The town was established as a trading post by Jacob Severin's company in 1741; it was named in his honor after the Battle of Jacobshavn, in which Danish forces defeated the Dutch whalers.
The final resident of nearby Sermermiut moved to Ilulissat in 1850. Ilulissat Declaration The town was the site of the Arctic Ocean Conference in May 2008.

history.text: It stated that the law of the sea provided for important rights and obligations concerning the delineation of the outer limits of the continental shelf, the protection of the marine environment (including ice-covered areas).

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It also said that it remained committed to this legal framework and to the orderly settlement of any possible overlapping claims.
